title: rubus orthostachyoides
intro: Rubus orthostachyoides, also known as Japanese Wineberry, is a species of raspberry native to East Asia and Japan, characterized by its vibrant red color and edible fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation. The health benefits of Rubus orthostachyoides include improving cardiovascular health, boosting the immune system, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, or used as a fresh fruit in jams, preserves, and other herbal preparations.
